name: Chargeable (Billable) Weight
description: The weight a carrier actually rates a shipment on: the greater of the actual (gross/scale) weight and the volumetric (dimensional) weight. Dense freight is billed on actual weight; light-but-bulky freight is billed on volumetric weight.

inputs.name: Volumetric weight
inputs.description: Dimensional weight from the carrier's divisor formula (see freight.volumetric_weight.divisor).

inputs.name: Actual gross weight
inputs.description: Scale weight of the shipment including packaging.

outputs.name: Chargeable weight
outputs.description: Weight the carrier bills on: the greater of actual and volumetric weight.

assumptions: Actual and volumetric weight are expressed in the same unit before comparing.
Volumetric weight is computed with the carrier's own current divisor.
No final-weight rounding is applied inside the formula; carriers typically round the billable weight up (e.g. UPS to the next whole pound).
limitations: Carriers may apply minimum billable weights, per-piece vs per-shipment comparison rules, and their own rounding before rating.
Sea/LCL freight is commonly rated per revenue tonne — the greater of cubic metres and metric tonnes — rather than by this parcel/air rule.
The comparison rule is stable across major carriers, but the volumetric side depends on divisors that change over time.

examples.note: Volumetric 1,600 kg vs actual 900 kg: the shipment is billed on the volumetric 1,600 kg.

sources: DHL eCommerce 'Chargeable Weight' business help page (dhl.com/us-en/home/ecommerce/business-help-center/chargeable-weight.html, checked 2026-07-02): chargeable weight is the greater of actual and dimensional weight.

FedEx 'What is dimensional weight?' page (fedex.com/en-us/shipping/packaging/what-is-dimensional-weight.html, checked 2026-07-02): shipments are charged on the greater of actual and dimensional weight.

UPS 'Shipping Dimensions and Weight' support page (ups.com/us/en/support/shipping-support/shipping-dimensions-weight, checked 2026-07-02): billable weight is the greater of dimensional and actual weight.
